title: Last Night on Earth - Soloing the Zombie Apocalypse!
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dv-40oFi5ak

Daniel (@dungeondive) gives a long, casual look at **Last Night on Earth: The Zombie Game** (10th Anniversary Edition, plus several expansions) — reframed as the solo/co-op game he's convinced it always should have been.

He backstory: a lifelong zombie-movie obsessive, he bought Flying Frog's debut in 2007 hoping to love it, but his group bounced hard off one flaw — nobody enjoyed the near-automated zombie side in its one-vs-many design. The fix, obvious in hindsight, is to play the zombies to the best of their ability and run it solo or co-op. Using community solo rules (Maniac Poodle's, lightly modified), it sings.

Most of the episode is an affectionate tour of Flying Frog's trademark abundance: a superb, top-10-worthy rulebook that compiles every expansion and the elegant "dots" system for balancing optional rules; a modular map of small-town buildings; huge hero and zombie card decks; grave weapons; exploration tokens; and enough randomizer bits to improvise scenarios on the fly. He shares his own tweaks (a trusty D4, heroes rising as zombies on death, tuned spawning) and plays a couple of sample turns. His register: think Return of the Living Dead zombies — cunning, not shambling. A resounding solo recommendation.

----

Daniel salvaged a game his group rejected by simply playing its weak "villain" side solo. Have you ever rescued a disappointing one-vs-many game by soloing or co-op-ing the AI yourself?
